# sourcevision

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 140
- **Lines of code**: 45,167
- **Languages**: TypeScript (127), JSON (7), Markdown (3), Other (3)
- **Zones**: 8 (8 with descriptions)
- **Git**: main @ 63d150a
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-05T03:18:39.152Z

## Architecture Zones

### Analysis Pipeline Core (`analysis-pipeline`)

Orchestrates the multi-phase codebase analysis pipeline including enrichment, context generation, zone detection output, and LLM integration.

- Files: 39
- Cohesion: 0.67, Coupling: 0.33
- Entry points: src/analyzers/callgraph-findings.ts, src/analyzers/claude-client.ts, src/analyzers/context.ts, src/analyzers/enrich-parsing.ts, src/analyzers/enrich.ts (+11 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/analysis-pipeline/context.md`

### Claude Code Configuration (`claude-config`)

Stores Claude Code editor settings and local overrides for the development environment.

- Files: 2
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/claude-config/context.md`

### CLI Interface & Command Layer (`cli-interface`)

Implements all user-facing CLI commands, output formatting, error handling, and manifest management for the sourcevision tool.

- Files: 26
- Cohesion: 0.71, Coupling: 0.29
- Entry points: src/analyzers/manifest.ts, src/cli/commands/init.ts, src/cli/commands/pr-markdown.ts, src/cli/errors.ts, src/cli/output.ts (+1 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/cli-interface/context.md`

### Core Algorithms & Schema (`core-algorithms`)

Houses the foundational algorithmic components — Louvain community detection, zone computation, branch classification, risk scoring, and the canonical schema definitions.

- Files: 33
- Cohesion: 0.56, Coupling: 0.44
- Entry points: src/analyzers/branch-work-classifier.ts, src/analyzers/risk-scoring.ts, src/analyzers/zones.ts, src/cli/commands/workspace.ts, src/cli/mcp.ts (+3 more)
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/core-algorithms/context.md`

### Integration & Validation Tests (`integration-validation-tests`)

Cross-cutting test zone grouping schema validation logic with e2e CLI tests and integration pipeline tests that exercise the full analysis workflow end-to-end.

- Files: 5
- Cohesion: 0.43, Coupling: 0.57
- Entry points: src/schema/validate.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/integration-validation-tests/context.md`

### Static Analyzer Services (`static-analyzer-services`)

Individual analyzer service implementations (callgraph, imports, inventory, components, route detection) and their path utilities, co-located with corresponding unit tests.

- Files: 11
- Cohesion: 0.54, Coupling: 0.46
- Entry points: src/analyzers/callgraph.ts, src/analyzers/components.ts, src/analyzers/imports.ts, src/analyzers/inventory.ts, src/util/paths.ts
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/static-analyzer-services/context.md`

### Test Fixture Projects (`test-fixture-projects`)

Self-contained sample codebases (a Remix app and a small TypeScript project) used as ground-truth inputs for e2e and integration tests of the analysis pipeline.

- Files: 11
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/test-fixture-projects/context.md`

### Workspace Root & Documentation (`workspace-root`)

Top-level monorepo scaffolding including documentation, build configuration, and workspace metadata.

- Files: 10
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/workspace-root/context.md`

## Key Dependencies

### Most-Imported Files

- `src/cli/output.ts` (14 imports)
- `src/util/sort.ts` (12 imports)
- `src/analyzers/claude-client.ts` (11 imports)
- `src/cli/errors.ts` (11 imports)
- `src/schema/data-files.ts` (10 imports)
- `src/analyzers/enrich-parsing.ts` (9 imports)
- `src/analyzers/zones.ts` (9 imports)
- `src/util/paths.ts` (9 imports)
- `src/analyzers/inventory.ts` (7 imports)
- `src/analyzers/token-usage.ts` (7 imports)

### External Packages

- `vitest` (used by 53 files)
- `@n-dx/llm-client` (used by 23 files)
- `typescript` (used by 5 files)
- `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk` (used by 2 files)
- `zod` (used by 2 files)
- `ora` (used by 1 files)
- `pdfkit` (used by 1 files)

### Circular Dependencies

Found 2 circular dependency chain(s):

- `src/analyzers/enrich.ts` → `src/analyzers/zones.ts`
- `src/analyzers/enrich.ts` → `src/analyzers/zones.ts`

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- The CLI zone receives 17 upward imports from three separate production zones (analyzers: 12, unit: 4, analyzers-2: 1), making it a de facto shared library rather than a leaf consumer. Any code in the CLI zone imported by upstream zones must be extracted to a lower-tier foundation module (e.g. src/output/, src/errors/) to restore the four-tier hierarchy documented in CLAUDE.md [critical]
- The src/analyzers/ directory contains files from four distinct architectural zones (analysis-pipeline, core-algorithms, cli-interface, analyzers-2) with no subdirectory structure; without filesystem-level zone separation, zone membership is only discoverable via tooling and accidental cross-zone imports are invisible during code review. [warning]
- analyzers → cli carries 12 imports — the single largest dependency inversion in the package — yet it has not been flagged at critical severity in prior enrichment passes. These 12 imports almost certainly represent shared output-formatting helpers or error types that belong in a foundation layer; until moved, every change to those CLI utilities risks breaking the analysis pipeline [critical]
- src/analyzers/claude-client.ts bypasses @n-dx/llm-client (the designated foundation-layer vendor abstraction) and imports the Anthropic SDK directly from within the domain layer; this creates a second untracked LLM vendor coupling path and undermines the architectural guarantee that vendor lock-in is isolated to the foundation layer. [warning]
- God function: registerMcpTools in src/cli/mcp.ts calls 36 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions [warning]

### Patterns

- analyzers-2 has a 15:7 import asymmetry with analysis-pipeline and participates in a three-way cycle (unit→analyzers→analyzers-2→unit via 2 return imports); consolidating analyzers-2 into analysis-pipeline would eliminate this cycle and reduce coupling [warning] (analyzers-2)
- Excluding the two violations, all cross-zone import edges flow strictly downward (fixtures → test → domain → cli), confirming the intended layered architecture is largely intact and the codebase is close to a fully clean dependency graph. [info]
- Production code in two zones imports from the e2e test zone; shared test helpers should be moved to a non-test package (e.g., src/test-utils/) to eliminate production→test dependencies [critical]
- Source zones importing from test zones (analyzers → e2e: 2, unit → e2e: 2) combined with the confirmed circular dependency count (2) strongly suggests these cross-boundary edges are the circular dependency chains — fixing them resolves both the layering violation and the circularity in one change. [critical]
- Three-zone circular import cycle detected: core-algorithms → analysis-pipeline → analyzers-2 → core-algorithms; circular zone-level dependencies prevent clean layering and must be broken by extracting the shared interface [critical]
- cli-interface functions as a shared output-formatting hub (88 total inbound calls across 2 upstream zones) despite being a leaf consumer by classification; a dedicated output-utilities zone should be extracted to make this responsibility explicit [warning]

### Relationships

- Circular dependency: core-algorithms and e2e zone mutually import from each other (2 imports each direction) — circular production↔test coupling must be resolved by extracting shared test utilities to a non-test location [critical] (e2e)
- unit→cli (4 imports) violates the four-tier dependency hierarchy defined in CLAUDE.md — foundational algorithm code must not import from the CLI presentation layer [critical]

### Observations

- 16 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports [warning] (analysis-pipeline)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (claude-config)
- 2 circular dependency chains detected — see imports.json for details [warning]
- Bidirectional coupling: "analysis-pipeline" ↔ "core-algorithms" (8+37 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Hub: src/analyzers/zones.ts is imported by 3 zones — cross-cutting dependency [info]
- Size imbalance: largest zone has 39 files vs smallest with 2 — uneven decomposition [info]
- Test fixture files (cohesion 1.0, coupling 0) and the Claude Code configuration zone (cohesion 1.0) represent the two cleanest zones in the package; the production source zones could target similar isolation by reducing the bidirectional coupling between analyzer layers. [info]
- The .claude and root zones are correctly inert — zero coupling means editor configuration and documentation cannot accidentally affect runtime behavior. [info]
- The CLI Interface zone has outbound imports to both analyzer zones and the unit/schema zone but receives very few inbound imports, consistent with its role as a terminal consumer — this is a healthy architectural pattern for a CLI entry layer. [info]
- The four runtime zones form a recognizable layered architecture: schema/algorithms (unit) → pipeline (analyzers, analyzers-2) → CLI (cli), with the bidirectional unit↔analyzers link being the one deviation from strict layering. [info]
- ... and 6 more

### Suggestions

- Zone "analysis-pipeline" has files across 9 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (analysis-pipeline)
- Add an src/analyzers/public.ts barrel as the single declared export surface for this zone, mirroring the package-level public.ts convention already used across all five packages; this caps the 16 entry points at a defined gate and makes undeclared cross-zone imports visible as convention violations. [warning] (analysis-pipeline)
- Rename completion-reader.ts to enrich-reader.ts (or enrich-completion.ts) to complete the enrich-* naming cluster with enrich-batch.ts and enrich-config.ts; the current name breaks the discoverable naming pattern and obscures that these three files form a coherent AI enrichment subsystem. [info] (analysis-pipeline)
- Zone "cli-interface" has files across 7 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (cli-interface)
- Before: analysis-pipeline and core-algorithms import directly from cli/output.ts and cli/errors.ts. After: create src/shared/output.ts and src/shared/errors.ts containing the exported symbols; cli/output.ts and cli/errors.ts re-export from src/shared/; upstream zones import from src/shared/ — CLI remains a leaf consumer with zero inbound production imports and the 17 upward dependency edges become legitimate downward imports. [warning] (cli-interface)
- Zone "core-algorithms" has files across 11 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (core-algorithms)
- Rename the zone to reflect its actual mixed content (e.g., 'algorithm-foundation') and enforce the name in architecture docs; the current name 'core-algorithms' causes reviewers to overlook misclassified non-algorithm files (workspace.ts, mcp.ts, branch-work-store.ts) because the name implies homogeneous algorithmic content. [info] (core-algorithms)
- core-algorithms has the worst cohesion/coupling ratio in the package (0.56 cohesion, 0.44 coupling) — it is the highest-fragility zone by metric and should be the primary stabilization target; all other refactoring work risks increasing this zone's instability further until its boundary is fixed. [critical] (core-algorithms)
- src/analyzers/ contains files from 4 zones (analysis-pipeline, cli-interface, core-algorithms, static-analyzer-services) — consider grouping into subdirectories to clarify architectural boundaries [info]
- No zone serves as a dedicated shared test infrastructure zone ('test-utils'). This single structural gap is the common root cause of two separately-flagged violations: production zones importing from test zones to access shared helpers, and src/schema/validate.ts being assigned to a test zone by community detection. Adding a src/test-utils/ source directory with its own zone would resolve both without touching any production runtime behavior. [warning]
- ... and 14 more
